text:
I was walking down a narrow alley. A chill prickled my spine. I felt something watching me.

That's when I saw her. An old woman crouching beside a doorway. She didn't look fearful. Instead, she seemed lost.

"What're you doing there?" I asked.

She didn't move.

"You look alone. Lost?"

She glanced my way. There was something oddly reassuring about her.

"I'm used to shadows. You?"

"I've lived here forever." I gestured. "You?"

"I call this mine."

I smiled warmly. It felt good. I sat beside her.

That afternoon, I taught her to find her way back home.

At evenings like these, I preferred old friends.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
